content.full_text:
18723(a)
General Counsel
JAMES E HOLST
Chief Associate Counsel

ASSOCIATE COUNSEL
Melvyn H. Gordon
George L. Marchand
Alerta R. Titmus

ASSISTANT COUNSEL
Romulus B. Portwood
Karl F. Dressel
David A. Darnnson
Glenn R. Woods
John F. Lundberg
Gary Morrison
Joseph L. Cowan, Jr.
Philip L. Spiegelman
Allen B. Wegner
James Richard, Jr.
Kate K. Alderman
Alice I. Youmans
Susan Amaleau
Lawrence B. Garcia

OFFICE OF THE GENERAL COUNSEL
550 University Hall
2200 University Avenue
Berkeley, California 94720
(415) 642-2822

November 15, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Proposed Amendment to Section 18723 --
     Dates for Filing Anniversary Statements
     of Economic Interests -- Political Reform
     Act of 1974

Dear Members of the Commission:

Assistant Counsel Kate Alderman of my office received a copy of the proposed amendment to Section 18723 from members of your Conflict of Interest Division Staff last Friday, November 11, at a meeting held at Berkeley regarding the University of California's proposed Conflict of Interest Code. Ms. Carla Bailey of your office has advised that the proposed amendment has been set for hearing this Thursday, November 17, 1977. I very much hope you will have time to receive and consider these comments before acting on the proposed amendment.

Section 18723(a) of the proposed regulation would, as amended, establish April 1 as the closing date for filing annual statements of economic interests under Article 2 of the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974. Because the provisions of Article 3 of those conflict of interest statutes require that reporting of financial interests be substantially similar to that required by Article 2, we assume that this regulation would apply to designated officials under the University's Conflict of Interest Code as well as to Article 2 filers.

As you know, the University of California has

Fair Political Practices Commission                                2.
November 15, 1977


established April 15 as the filing date for annual financial
disclosure statements in its Conflict of Interest Code,
which is presently being reviewed by the Commission. On
behalf of the University, and at the risk of repeating
comments made during the Commission's public hearing on the
University of California Code, I again urge that April 15 be
adopted as the closing date for the filing of these annual
statements. The reason for this recommendation, as I indicated
at the September 4, 1977 hearing on the University's proposed
code, is that April 15 corresponds to the deadline for the
filing of Federal and State income tax returns. The use of an
identical date in this regulation would, I believe, be most
helpful to designated officials in reviewing their financial
holdings. It would also be a date which could be easily
remembered by filers because of its coinciding with the date
for filing tax returns.

     I do not believe that agencies whose codes presently
contain an April 1 deadline would be prejudiced by use of a
later filing date in this proposed amendment, but rather would
be free to liberalize their own codes and thereby to take
advantage of the significant and practical benefits associated
with the April 15 deadline.

     Thank you in advance for your consideration of these
comments.


                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Donald L. Feidnaar
                                        General Counsel

cc: Ted Prima, Esq.
    FPPC Legal Division

    President Saxon
    Vice President Swain
    Vice President Kleingartner

sections.question_synthetic: Should the Fair Political Practices Commission adopt April 15 as the closing date for filing annual statements of economic interests, rather than other proposed dates?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence from UC to the FPPC, not an FPPC response or advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document advocates for a specific policy position regarding filing deadlines for conflict of interest disclosures.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Should the Fair Political Practices Commission adopt April 15 as the closing date for filing annual statements of economic interests, rather than other proposed dates?
embedding.first_500_words: This is a letter from the Office of the General Counsel at the University of California to the Fair Political Practices Commission regarding a proposed amendment to Section 18723 of the Political Reform Act of 1974. The letter discusses the proposed amendment's impact on the closing date for filing annual statements of economic interests under Article 2 of the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974. It also mentions that the proposed amendment has been set for hearing and encourages the Commission to consider the University's comments before acting on the proposed amendment. The letter is signed by Assistant Counsel Kate Alderman and includes a list of other counselors and associates in the Office of the General Counsel. Fair Political Practices Commission November 15, 1977 established April 15 as the filing date for annual financial disclosure statements in its Conflict of Interest Code, which is presently being reviewed by the Commission. On behalf of the University, and at the risk of repeating comments made during the Commission's public hearings on the University of California Code, I again urge that April 15 be adopted as the closing date for the filing of these annual statements. The reason for this recommendation, as I indicated at the September 4, 1977 hearing on the University's proposed code, is that April 15 corresponds to the deadline for the filing of federal and state income tax returns. The use of an identical date in this regulation would, I believe, be most helpful to designated officials in reviewing their financial holdings. It would also be a date which could be easily remembered by filers because of its coinciding with the date for filing tax returns. I do not believe that agencies whose codes presently contain an April 1 deadline would be prejudiced by use of a later filing date in this proposed amendment, but rather would be free to liberalize their own codes and thereby to take advantage of the significant and practical benefits associated with the April 15 deadline. Thank you in advance for your consideration of these comments. Sincerely, Donald L. Reidhaar General Counsel cc: Ted Primm, Esq. FPPC Legal Division President Saxon Vice President Swain Vice President Kleingartner
embedding.summary: The University of California General Counsel submits comments to the FPPC urging adoption of April 15 as the filing deadline for annual financial disclosure statements, arguing that this date aligns with federal and state income tax filing deadlines and would benefit designated officials.
